content_en:
In Zaad al-Maseer, ibn al-Jauzi stated, “The verse indicates that it is allowed to have a marriage contract without stating the dower.”?

It is confirmed in the sunnah from Ugqbah ibn Aamir who narrated that the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) said to a man, “Would you be pleased if I were to marry you to so and so?” He said, “Yes.” Then he said to the woman, “Would you be pleased if I were to marry you to so and so?” She said, “Yes.” So he married the two of them together. Uqbah consummated the marriage and did not establish any dower for her. When he was about to die, he said, “The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) married me to so and so and he did not state a dower for her and I did not give her anything. Therefore, I give her as the dower my share [of the war booty] in Khaibar.” She took it and sold it for two hundred thousand [dinars].

' Kashaaf al-Qinaa, vol. 5, p. 144; al-Badaai, vol. 2, p. 274; al-Mubadbib, vol. 2, pp. 55 and 60; al-Sharh al-Sagheer, vol. 2, P 449; and al-Figh al-Islaami wa 4dillatubu by Wahba al-Zuhaili, vol. 7, pp. 23 and 254. 5 Zaad al-Maseer, vol. 1, p. 279.

Recorded by Abu Dawood (#2117), The Book on Marriage, Chapter on one who marries and did not determine a dower until he dies. Its chain is basan. Al-Haakim

‘The Figh of Marriage

Based on that, “if two spouses agree to get married without a dower, the marriage is valid according to the majority of the scholars, save the Malikis. When the marriage is consummated or when the man dies, she has the right to a dower that is comparable to the dower given to those women who are similar to her. According to the Malikis, if two agree to get married with a dower, the marriage is not valid.”!

To state the dower at the time of the marriage contract is neither an essential component nor a condition for the validity of the marriage contract. This is because the dower is a resultant ruling that follows from the contract and it is not necessary to mention the rulings that are the result of the contract when the contract is actually being stated. This is also based on the previous evidence given. In al-Mughni it states, “Marriage is sound without the stating of the dower according to the vast majority of the scholars.”2

However, ibn Taimiya has concluded that one should state the dower at the time of the marriage contract in order to avoid any dispute or confrontation. He also concluded that the dower is an essential component of the marriage and that the dower must be mentioned or she is to be given what is given to women similar to her. He wrote,

Those who state that the dower is not a goal in itself are making a statement that has no truth to it. It is, in fact, an essential component of the marriage and it being a condition of it makes it a more important condition than the price [in a sale] for the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) said, “The conditions that have the most right to be fulfilled are those conditions that make the private

recorded it in al-Mustadrak (vol. 2, p. 182), and he declared it sabib. Al-Dhahabi greed with his assessment.

Bidaayah al-Mujtahid, vol. 2, p. 52. Al-Mughni, vol. 6, pp. 680-681.

‘The Figh of Marriage

parts lawful.” Wealth is permissible in exchange for other wealth. However, the private parts are not permissible for another except with the dower. A marriage can be enacted without stating or fixing the amount of dower but it cannot be enacted while negating the dower. For the marriage in which the dower is not stated, the dower then becomes the dower ofa similar woman... What is confirmed in the Quran, sunnah and consensus is that a marriage can be enacted without fixing the dower. But it cannot be enacted while negating the dower. In fact, Allah has_ said, “Indeed, We know what We have enjoined upon them concerning their wives and whom their right hands possess” (al-Ahzaab 50). [Since the preceding portion of the verse] states that the Prophet (peace be upon him) may marry without a dower, it was made obligatory upon them to marry with a dower. This is what the Quran indicates in more than one place. There must be either a stated dower or a non-stated dower [but there cannot be a marriage if there is a refusal to accept the payment of a dower].?

Therefore, there are three possible scenarios:

One case is where the two agree upon not having a dower. This is not allowed and such a marriage contract is not valid.

A. second case is where the dower is clearly stated at the time of the marriage contract. This is the normal, most complete [and best] case.

A third case is where the dower is not mentioned at the time of the contract. This is permissible and the women will then receive what women similar to her receive or whatever they may later agree upon.

' Recorded by al-Bukhari (#5151), The Book on Conditions, Chapter on conditions for the dower at the time of the marriage contract, and by Muslim (#1418), The Book on Marriage, Chapter on fulfilling conditions and marriage.

Majmoo Fatawa Shaikh al-Islaam ibn Taimiya, vol. 29, . 344. [The above is not a translation of what is found in Sadlaan’s work because the text has typographical errors that completely distort the meaning of ibn Taimiya’s words. Hence, the passage was translated directly from the new edition of Majmoo Fatawa, vol. 15, part

The Figh of Marriage

The Maximum Amount of Dower Allowed!

The jurists agree that there is no maximum limit to the dower, since there is nothing stated in the shareeah mentioning an upper limit. Allah says in the Quran,

CO wae oe 9 ee eo mE Wythe CANE) BIT g 95 OKA g 93 lteseal S94! OLy Viet ly Vid 4 ET ee ade 1 yb “But if you intend to replace a wife by another and you have given one a qintaar (large amount of gold), take not the least bit back of it. Would you take it wrongfully without a right and in manifest sin?” (al-Nisaa 20).

The mention of a qintaar is not meant to place an upper limit to the dower but it is simply “a figurative expression implying a great amount.” It that were meant to state the greater limit to the dower, Allah would have prohibited us from going beyond that.

The Minimum Amount Allowable for a Dower

Concerning the minimum amount permissible for a dower, there are five well-known opinions.

' The jurists have stated the maximum and minimum allowed dower in terms of dirhams and dinars because they were the monetary units common among the people in the past. Therefore, it would be to discuss their origins and their weights in modern terms. Dirham is originally a Greek work, drachma, and it is a word for a silver coin that is equivalent to seven-tenths of a dinar. Therefore, in order to know the weight of a dirham, one must know the weight of a dinar. Dinar comes from a Latin-Greek term, dinariyus, and it was one of the most important coins used in the Islamic lands. Its weight in grams is 4.25 s. Since a dirham is seventenths of that, its weight is 2.975 grams. See Figh al-Zakaat by Dr. Yusuf alaradhawi, vol. 1, p. 259 (Muassasat al-Risalah, Lebanon, eighth edition, 1405 A.H.). Sadan’ yen actual states 9.975, but that is obviously simply a typographical Or —

Nail al-Maraam min Tafseer Ayaat al-Abkaam by Muhammad Sideeq Khaan, p. 137 (Daar al-Raaid, Lebanon).

‘The Figh of Marriage

The first opinion is that a proper dower cannot be less than ten dirhams. This is the opinion of the Hanafis.1

The second opinion is that the minimum acceptable for a dower is three dirhams, a quarter of a dinar or what is equivalent in value to that of goods or of something pure and not impure, something that is considered wealth, assets or property from a shareeah perspective, something that may be permissibly benefited from, and not like a lute [which has no value according to the shareeah]. It must be something that one can hand over to the wife, whose amount, type and term is known and fixed. This is the opinion of the Malikis.?

A third opinion is that the dower is valid as long as anything which can be called wealth or its equivalent is given, as long as the parties mutually consent to it. This is the opinion of the Shafi’ees, Hanbalis, ibn Wahb of the Malikis, Ishaaq ibn Rahawaih, Abu Thaur, the jurists of the Madinah at the time of the Followers, al-Hasan alBasri, al-Thauri, al-Auzaa’ee and Saeed ibn al-Musayyab.3

A fourth opinion is that anything which may be called “a thing”, even if it is just a grain of barley, is acceptable as the dower. This was the opinion of ibn Hazm.*

A fifth opinion is that the dower is valid by anything which has value, either material or non-material.

Those of the first opinion, that the minimum dower is ten dirhams, support their view with the following evidence:

First is what is recorded by al-Daaraqutni and al-Baihaqi from Jaabir ibn Abdullah that the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) said,

3 Fath al-Qadeer, vol. 2, pp. 435-436; Nail al-Autaar, vol. 6, p. 167.

3Al. Shar! al Saghecr, vol. 2, p. 28; al-Mudawanab al-Kubra, vol. 2, pp. 173-174. 3 Ab. I-Majmoo Sharh al-Mubadbib, vol. 5, ' 482.

fi -Muballa Sharh al-Majallab by ibn Hazm, the Dhaahiri, vol. 11, pp. 96-97, issue 1850.

"The Figh of Marriage

a8 Vy USN oy VY crags Vy slg VY] cles oy Y vi ple BRE oy Jil

“No one is to marry women off except the guardians. They are not to be married except to those who are suitable. There is no dower less than ten dirhams.”!

This hadith has been objected to because it is weak. Therefore, it is not proper to use it as evidence. It was narrated by Mubashir ibn Ubaid, and he is weak, from al-Hajaaj ibn Arta‘ah and he committed tadlees.?

Second is what is recorded by al-Baihaqi through the chain of Shuraik on the authority of Dawood al-Audee on the authority of alShaabi on the authority of Ali (may Allah be pleased with him) who said, “The minimum that makes the private parts [of the wife] permissible is ten dirhams.” This proof is refuted because Dawood alAudee is not trustworthy. Ibn Maeen said about him, “He is not worth anything.”
content_id:
Dalam Zaad al-Maseer, Ibnu al-Jauzi menyatakan, "Ayat ini menunjukkan bahwa diperbolehkan melangsungkan akad nikah tanpa menyebutkan mahar."

Hal ini dikuatkan dalam sunnah dari Uqbah ibn Aamir yang meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) berkata kepada seorang laki-laki, "Apakah kamu rela jika aku menikahkanmu dengan si fulanah?" Ia menjawab, "Ya." Kemudian beliau berkata kepada perempuan tersebut, "Apakah kamu rela jika aku menikahkanmu dengan si fulan?" Ia menjawab, "Ya." Maka beliau menikahkan keduanya. Uqbah menggauli istrinya dan tidak menetapkan mahar untuknya. Ketika hendak wafat, ia berkata, "Rasulullah (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) menikahkan aku dengan si fulanah dan beliau tidak menyebutkan mahar untuknya, dan aku pun tidak memberikan sesuatu kepadanya. Maka aku jadikan sebagai maharnya bagianku [dari harta rampasan perang] di Khaibar." Ia pun mengambilnya dan menjualnya seharga dua ratus ribu [dinar].

' Kashaaf al-Qinaa, jil. 5, hlm. 144; al-Badaai, jil. 2, hlm. 274; al-Mubadzbib, jil. 2, hlm. 55 dan 60; al-Sharh al-Shagheer, jil. 2, hlm. 449; dan al-Fiqh al-Islami wa Adillatuhu karya Wahba al-Zuhaili, jil. 7, hlm. 23 dan 254. 5 Zaad al-Maseer, jil. 1, hlm. 279.

Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Dawud (#2117), Kitab Nikah, Bab tentang orang yang menikah tanpa menetapkan mahar hingga ia wafat. Sanadnya hasan. Al-Hakim

'Fiqh Pernikahan

Berdasarkan hal itu, "jika kedua pasangan sepakat untuk menikah tanpa mahar, maka pernikahan tersebut sah menurut mayoritas ulama, kecuali ulama Malikiyah. Apabila pernikahan telah dikonsumasi atau suami meninggal dunia, maka istri berhak mendapatkan mahar mitsil, yaitu mahar yang setara dengan mahar yang diberikan kepada wanita-wanita yang serupa dengannya. Menurut ulama Malikiyah, jika keduanya sepakat menikah dengan mahar, maka pernikahan tersebut tidak sah."

Menyebutkan mahar pada saat akad nikah bukanlah syarat rukun maupun syarat sahnya akad nikah. Hal ini karena mahar merupakan hukum yang lahir dari akad, dan tidak wajib menyebutkan hukum-hukum yang merupakan konsekuensi dari akad ketika akad itu diucapkan. Hal ini juga didasarkan pada dalil-dalil yang telah disebutkan sebelumnya. Dalam al-Mughni dinyatakan, "Pernikahan sah tanpa penyebutan mahar menurut mayoritas besar ulama."

Namun demikian, Ibnu Taimiyah menyimpulkan bahwa mahar hendaknya disebutkan pada saat akad nikah guna menghindari perselisihan dan konfrontasi. Beliau juga menyimpulkan bahwa mahar merupakan rukun pernikahan dan mahar harus disebutkan, atau jika tidak disebutkan maka ia diberikan mahar mitsil sebagaimana yang diberikan kepada wanita-wanita yang serupa dengannya. Beliau menulis,

Mereka yang menyatakan bahwa mahar bukanlah tujuan itu sendiri sesungguhnya membuat pernyataan yang tidak benar. Mahar adalah rukun pernikahan, dan menjadikannya sebagai syarat membuatnya lebih penting dari harga [dalam jual beli], karena Rasulullah (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) bersabda, "Syarat yang paling berhak untuk dipenuhi adalah syarat yang menghalalkan

dicatat dalam al-Mustadrak (jil. 2, hlm. 182), dan beliau menyatakannya shahih. Al-Dzahabi sependapat dengan penilaiannya.

Bidayah al-Mujtahid, jil. 2, hlm. 52. Al-Mughni, jil. 6, hlm. 680-681.

'Fiqh Pernikahan

kemaluan." Harta boleh ditukar dengan harta lainnya. Namun kemaluan tidak halal bagi orang lain kecuali dengan mahar. Suatu pernikahan dapat dilangsungkan tanpa menyebutkan atau menetapkan jumlah mahar, tetapi tidak dapat dilangsungkan dengan meniadakan mahar sama sekali. Untuk pernikahan yang maharnya tidak disebutkan, maka maharnya menjadi mahar mitsil... Yang telah dikonfirmasi dalam Quran, sunnah, dan ijma' adalah bahwa pernikahan dapat dilangsungkan tanpa menetapkan mahar. Namun tidak dapat dilangsungkan dengan meniadakan mahar. Bahkan Allah berfirman, "Sesungguhnya Kami mengetahui apa yang Kami wajibkan kepada mereka tentang istri-istri mereka dan hamba sahaya yang mereka miliki" (al-Ahzab: 50). [Karena bagian sebelumnya dari ayat tersebut] menyatakan bahwa Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) boleh menikah tanpa mahar, maka diwajibkan atas mereka untuk menikah dengan mahar. Inilah yang ditunjukkan oleh Quran di lebih dari satu tempat. Harus ada mahar yang disebutkan atau mahar yang tidak disebutkan [tetapi pernikahan tidak dapat dilangsungkan jika ada penolakan untuk membayar mahar].

Oleh karena itu, terdapat tiga skenario yang mungkin terjadi:

Kasus pertama adalah ketika keduanya sepakat untuk tidak ada mahar. Hal ini tidak diperbolehkan dan akad nikah semacam itu tidak sah.

Kasus kedua adalah ketika mahar disebutkan secara jelas pada saat akad nikah. Inilah kasus yang normal, paling sempurna [dan terbaik].

Kasus ketiga adalah ketika mahar tidak disebutkan pada saat akad. Hal ini diperbolehkan dan wanita tersebut akan mendapatkan apa yang diterima oleh wanita-wanita yang serupa dengannya, atau apa pun yang mereka sepakati kemudian.

' Diriwayatkan oleh al-Bukhari (#5151), Kitab Syarat-syarat, Bab tentang syarat-syarat mahar pada saat akad nikah, dan oleh Muslim (#1418), Kitab Nikah, Bab tentang memenuhi syarat-syarat dalam pernikahan.

Majmu' Fatawa Syaikh al-Islam Ibnu Taimiyah, jil. 29, hlm. 344. [Di atas bukan merupakan terjemahan dari apa yang terdapat dalam karya Sadlan karena teks tersebut mengandung kesalahan cetak yang sepenuhnya mendistorsi makna kata-kata Ibnu Taimiyah. Oleh karena itu, kutipan ini diterjemahkan langsung dari edisi baru Majmu' Fatawa, jil. 15, bagian

Fiqh Pernikahan

Jumlah Maksimum Mahar yang Diperbolehkan

Para fuqaha sepakat bahwa tidak ada batas maksimum mahar, karena tidak ada dalil dalam syariat yang menyebutkan batas atas. Allah berfirman dalam Quran,

CO wae oe 9 ee eo mE Wythe CANE) BIT g 95 OKA g 93 lteseal S94! OLy Viet ly Vid 4 ET ee ade 1 yb "Dan jika kamu ingin mengganti istrimu dengan istri yang lain, sedang kamu telah memberikan kepada seseorang di antara mereka harta yang banyak (qinthar), maka janganlah kamu mengambil kembali sedikitpun darinya. Apakah kamu akan mengambilnya kembali dengan cara yang tidak benar dan dengan dosa yang nyata?" (al-Nisa: 20).

Penyebutan qinthar tidak dimaksudkan untuk menetapkan batas atas mahar, melainkan hanya "ungkapan kiasan yang menyiratkan jumlah yang sangat besar." Jika hal itu dimaksudkan untuk menyatakan batas tertinggi mahar, niscaya Allah akan melarang kita melampaui jumlah tersebut.

Jumlah Minimum yang Diperbolehkan untuk Mahar

Mengenai jumlah minimum yang diperbolehkan untuk mahar, terdapat lima pendapat yang masyhur.

' Para fuqaha menyebutkan jumlah maksimum dan minimum mahar dalam satuan dirham dan dinar karena keduanya merupakan satuan mata uang yang umum digunakan masyarakat pada masa lampau. Oleh karena itu, penting untuk membahas asal-usul dan bobotnya dalam istilah modern. Dirham pada asalnya berasal dari bahasa Yunani, drachma, dan merupakan sebutan untuk koin perak yang setara dengan tujuh per sepuluh dinar. Oleh karena itu, untuk mengetahui berat dirham, seseorang harus mengetahui berat dinar. Dinar berasal dari istilah Latin-Yunani, dinariyus, dan merupakan salah satu koin terpenting yang digunakan di wilayah Islam. Beratnya dalam gram adalah 4,25 gram. Karena dirham adalah tujuh per sepuluh dari itu, maka beratnya adalah 2,975 gram. Lihat Fiqh al-Zakat karya Dr. Yusuf al-Qaradhawi, jil. 1, hlm. 259 (Muassasat al-Risalah, Lebanon, edisi kedelapan, 1405 H.). Sadlan menyebutkan angka 9,975, namun itu jelas hanya kesalahan cetak.

Nail al-Maram min Tafsir Ayat al-Ahkam karya Muhammad Shiddiq Khan, hlm. 137 (Dar al-Ra'id, Lebanon).

'Fiqh Pernikahan

Pendapat pertama adalah bahwa mahar yang layak tidak boleh kurang dari sepuluh dirham. Ini adalah pendapat ulama Hanafiyah.

Pendapat kedua adalah bahwa jumlah minimum yang dapat diterima untuk mahar adalah tiga dirham, seperempat dinar, atau yang setara nilainya berupa barang atau sesuatu yang suci dan bukan najis, sesuatu yang dianggap sebagai harta, aset, atau properti dari perspektif syariat, sesuatu yang boleh dimanfaatkan, dan bukan seperti alat musik senar [yang tidak memiliki nilai menurut syariat]. Mahar haruslah sesuatu yang dapat diserahkan kepada istri, yang jumlah, jenis, dan waktu penyerahannya diketahui dan ditetapkan. Ini adalah pendapat ulama Malikiyah.

Pendapat ketiga adalah bahwa mahar sah selama sesuatu yang dapat disebut harta atau yang setara dengannya diberikan, asalkan para pihak saling menyetujuinya. Ini adalah pendapat ulama Syafi'iyah, Hanabilah, Ibnu Wahb dari kalangan Malikiyah, Ishaq ibn Rahawaih, Abu Thaur, para fuqaha Madinah pada masa Tabi'in, al-Hasan al-Bashri, al-Tsauri, al-Auzaa'i, dan Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab.

Pendapat keempat adalah bahwa apa pun yang dapat disebut "sesuatu", meskipun hanya sebutir gandum, dapat diterima sebagai mahar. Ini adalah pendapat ibn Hazm.*

Pendapat kelima adalah bahwa mahar sah dengan apa pun yang memiliki nilai, baik material maupun non-material.

Mereka yang berpendapat pertama, bahwa mahar minimum adalah sepuluh dirham, mendasarkan pandangan mereka dengan bukti berikut:

Pertama adalah apa yang dicatat oleh al-Daaraqutni dan al-Baihaqi dari Jaabir ibn Abdullah bahwa Rasulullah (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) bersabda,

3 Fath al-Qadeer, vol. 2, pp. 435-436; Nail al-Autaar, vol. 6, p. 167.

3Al. Shar! al Saghecr, vol. 2, p. 28; al-Mudawanab al-Kubra, vol. 2, pp. 173-174. 3 Ab. I-Majmoo Sharh al-Mubadbib, vol. 5, ' 482.

fi -Muballa Sharh al-Majallab by ibn Hazm, the Dhaahiri, vol. 11, pp. 96-97, issue 1850.

"Fiqh Pernikahan

a8 Vy USN oy VY crags Vy slg VY] cles oy Y vi ple BRE oy Jil

"Tidak seorang pun boleh menikahkan wanita kecuali para wali. Mereka tidak boleh dinikahkan kecuali dengan orang yang sepadan. Tidak ada mahar yang kurang dari sepuluh dirham."!

Hadith ini telah dikritik karena ia dha'if. Oleh karena itu, tidak tepat menggunakannya sebagai dalil. Hadith ini diriwayatkan oleh Mubashir ibn Ubaid, dan ia dha'if, dari al-Hajaaj ibn Arta'ah yang melakukan tadlis.?

Kedua adalah apa yang dicatat oleh al-Baihaqi melalui sanad Shuraik dari Dawood al-Audee dari al-Shaabi dari Ali (radhiyallahu 'anhu) yang berkata, "Minimum yang menghalalkan kemaluan [istri] adalah sepuluh dirham." Dalil ini ditolak karena Dawood al-Audee tidak tsiqah. Ibn Maeen berkata tentang dirinya, "Ia tidak bernilai apa pun."
content_ar:
In Zaad al-Maseer, ibn al-Jauzi stated, “The verse indicates that it is allowed to have a marriage contract without stating the dower.”?

It is confirmed in the السنة from Ugqbah ibn Aamir who narrated that الرسول ﷺ of الله (عليه السلام) said to a man, “Would you be pleased if I were to marry you to so and so?” He said, “Yes.” Then he said to the woman, “Would you be pleased if I were to marry you to so and so?” She said, “Yes.” So he married the two of them together. Uqbah consummated the marriage and did not establish any dower for her. When he was about to die, he said, “الرسول ﷺ of الله (عليه السلام) married me to so and so and he did not state a dower for her and I did not give her anything. Therefore, I give her as the dower my share [of the war booty] in Khaibar.” She took it and sold it for two hundred thousand [dinars].

' Kashaaf al-Qinaa, vol. 5, p. 144; al-Badaai, vol. 2, p. 274; al-Mubadbib, vol. 2, pp. 55 and 60; al-Sharh al-Sagheer, vol. 2, P 449; and al-Figh al-Islaami wa 4dillatubu by Wahba al-Zuhaili, vol. 7, pp. 23 and 254. 5 Zaad al-Maseer, vol. 1, p. 279.

Recorded by Abu Dawood (#2117), The Book on Marriage, Chapter on one who marries and did not determine a dower until he dies. Its chain is basan. Al-Haakim

‘The Figh of Marriage

Based on that, “if two spouses agree to get married without a dower, the marriage is valid according to the majority of the scholars, save the Malikis. When the marriage is consummated or when the man dies, she has the right to a dower that is comparable to the dower given to those women who are similar to her. According to the Malikis, if two agree to get married with a dower, the marriage is not valid.”!

To state the dower at the time of the marriage contract is neither an essential component nor a condition for the validity of the marriage contract. This is because the dower is a resultant ruling that follows from the contract and it is not necessary to mention the rulings that are the result of the contract when the contract is actually being stated. This is also based on the previous evidence given. In al-Mughni it states, “Marriage is sound without the stating of the dower according to the vast majority of the scholars.”2

However, ibn Taimiya has concluded that one should state the dower at the time of the marriage contract in order to avoid any dispute or confrontation. He also concluded that the dower is an essential component of the marriage and that the dower must be mentioned or she is to be given what is given to women similar to her. He wrote,

Those who state that the dower is not a goal in itself are making a statement that has no truth to it. It is, in fact, an essential component of the marriage and it being a condition of it makes it a more important condition than the price [in a sale] for الرسول ﷺ of الله (عليه السلام) said, “The conditions that have the most right to be fulfilled are those conditions that make the private

recorded it in al-Mustadrak (vol. 2, p. 182), and he declared it sabib. Al-Dhahabi greed with his assessment.

Bidaayah al-Mujtahid, vol. 2, p. 52. Al-Mughni, vol. 6, pp. 680-681.

‘The Figh of Marriage

parts lawful.” Wealth is permissible in exchange for other wealth. However, the private parts are not permissible for another except with the dower. A marriage can be enacted without stating or fixing the amount of dower but it cannot be enacted while negating the dower. For the marriage in which the dower is not stated, the dower then becomes the dower ofa similar woman... What is confirmed in the القرآن, السنة and consensus is that a marriage can be enacted without fixing the dower. But it cannot be enacted while negating the dower. In fact, الله has_ said, “Indeed, We know what We have enjoined upon them concerning their wives and whom their right hands possess” (al-Ahzaab 50). [Since the preceding portion of the verse] states that النبي ﷺ (عليه السلام) may marry without a dower, it was made obligatory upon them to marry with a dower. This is what the القرآن indicates in more than one place. There must be either a stated dower or a non-stated dower [but there cannot be a marriage if there is a refusal to accept the payment of a dower].?

Therefore, there are three possible scenarios:

One case is where the two agree upon not having a dower. This is not allowed and such a marriage contract is not valid.

A. second case is where the dower is clearly stated at the time of the marriage contract. This is the normal, most complete [and best] case.

A third case is where the dower is not mentioned at the time of the contract. This is permissible and the women will then receive what women similar to her receive or whatever they may later agree upon.

' Recorded by al-Bukhari (#5151), The Book on Conditions, Chapter on conditions for the dower at the time of the marriage contract, and by المسلمين (#1418), The Book on Marriage, Chapter on fulfilling conditions and marriage.

Majmoo Fatawa Shaikh al-Islaam ibn Taimiya, vol. 29, . 344. [The above is not a translation of what is found in Sadlaan’s work because the text has typographical errors that completely distort the meaning of ibn Taimiya’s words. Hence, the passage was translated directly from the new edition of Majmoo Fatawa, vol. 15, part

The Figh of Marriage

The Maximum Amount of Dower Allowed!

The jurists agree that there is no maximum limit to the dower, since there is nothing stated in the shareeah mentioning an upper limit. الله says in the القرآن,

CO wae oe 9 ee eo mE Wythe CANE) BIT g 95 OKA g 93 lteseal S94! OLy Viet ly Vid 4 ET ee ade 1 yb “But if you intend to replace a wife by another and you have given one a qintaar (large amount of gold), take not the least bit back of it. Would you take it wrongfully without a right and in manifest sin?” (al-Nisaa 20).

The mention of a qintaar is not meant to place an upper limit to the dower but it is simply “a figurative expression implying a great amount.” It that were meant to state the greater limit to the dower, الله would have prohibited us from going beyond that.

The Minimum Amount Allowable for a Dower

Concerning the minimum amount permissible for a dower, there are five well-known opinions.

' The jurists have stated the maximum and minimum allowed dower in terms of dirhams and dinars because they were the monetary units common among the people in the past. Therefore, it would be to discuss their origins and their weights in modern terms. Dirham is originally a Greek work, drachma, and it is a word for a silver coin that is equivalent to seven-tenths of a dinar. Therefore, in order to know the weight of a dirham, one must know the weight of a dinar. Dinar comes from a Latin-Greek term, dinariyus, and it was one of the most important coins used in the Islamic lands. Its weight in grams is 4.25 s. Since a dirham is seventenths of that, its weight is 2.975 grams. See Figh al-Zakaat by Dr. Yusuf alaradhawi, vol. 1, p. 259 (Muassasat al-Risalah, Lebanon, eighth edition, 1405 A.H.). Sadan’ yen actual states 9.975, but that is obviously simply a typographical Or —

Nail al-Maraam min Tafseer Ayaat al-Abkaam by Muhammad Sideeq Khaan, p. 137 (Daar al-Raaid, Lebanon).

‘The Figh of Marriage

The first opinion is that a proper dower cannot be less than ten dirhams. This is the opinion of the Hanafis.1

The second opinion is that the minimum acceptable for a dower is three dirhams, a quarter of a dinar or what is equivalent in value to that of goods or of something pure and not impure, something that is considered wealth, assets or property from a shareeah perspective, something that may be permissibly benefited from, and not like a lute [which has no value according to the shareeah]. It must be something that one can hand over to the wife, whose amount, type and term is known and fixed. This is the opinion of the Malikis.?

A third opinion is that the dower is valid as long as anything which can be called wealth or its equivalent is given, as long as the parties mutually consent to it. This is the opinion of the Shafi’ees, Hanbalis, ibn Wahb of the Malikis, Ishaaq ibn Rahawaih, Abu Thaur, the jurists of the Madinah at the time of the Followers, al-Hasan alBasri, al-Thauri, al-Auzaa’ee and Saeed ibn al-Musayyab.3

A fourth opinion is that anything which may be called “a thing”, even if it is just a grain of barley, is acceptable as the dower. This was the opinion of ibn Hazm.*

A fifth opinion is that the dower is valid by anything which has value, either material or non-material.

Those of the first opinion, that the minimum dower is ten dirhams, support their view with the following evidence:

First is what is recorded by al-Daaraqutni and al-Baihaqi from Jaabir ibn Abdullah that الرسول ﷺ of الله (عليه السلام) said,

3 Fath al-Qadeer, vol. 2, pp. 435-436; Nail al-Autaar, vol. 6, p. 167.

3Al. Shar! al Saghecr, vol. 2, p. 28; al-Mudawanab al-Kubra, vol. 2, pp. 173-174. 3 Ab. I-Majmoo Sharh al-Mubadbib, vol. 5, ' 482.

fi -Muballa Sharh al-Majallab by ibn Hazm, the Dhaahiri, vol. 11, pp. 96-97, issue 1850.

"The Figh of Marriage

a8 Vy USN oy VY crags Vy slg VY] cles oy Y vi ple BRE oy Jil

“No one is to marry women off except the guardians. They are not to be married except to those who are suitable. There is no dower less than ten dirhams.”!

This الحديث has been objected to because it is weak. Therefore, it is not proper to use it as evidence. It was narrated by Mubashir ibn Ubaid, and he is weak, from al-Hajaaj ibn Arta‘ah and he committed tadlees.?

Second is what is recorded by al-Baihaqi through the chain of Shuraik on the authority of Dawood al-Audee on the authority of alShaabi on the authority of Ali (may الله be pleased with him) who said, “The minimum that makes the private parts [of the wife] permissible is ten dirhams.” This proof is refuted because Dawood alAudee is not trustworthy. Ibn Maeen said about him, “He is not worth anything.”
